title: clematis acuminata subsp. andersonii
intro: Clematis acuminata subsp. andersonii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, native to the Himalayas and parts of India. It is a climbing plant with attractive white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots of Clematis acuminata subsp. andersonii are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders for medicinal purposes.
